zh.lifeSummary: 當Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn於1196年出生在英國威爾斯蒙格莫利夏爾特雷農時，他的父親Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd37歲，母親Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain40歲。他於大約1240年與Alswn verch Maredudd在威爾士蒙格莫利夏爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和6個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn于1196年出生在英国威尔斯蒙格莫利夏爾特雷農时，他的父亲Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd37岁，母亲Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain40岁。他于大约1240年与Alswn verch Maredudd在威尔士蒙格莫利夏爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和6个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Meilir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Fritz.

variants.name: Meile
meanings: Swiss German, German, and Danish: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names formed with mag- ‘might’ or māg ‘kinsman’ as the first element.

nameMeanings.name: Gryg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Gregg
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): from the Middle English personal name Greg, a pet form of Gregory ; compare Grigg .

nameMeanings.name: Llewellyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

variants.name: Llywellyn
meanings: Traditional: altered form (influenced by llew ‘lion’) of Llywelyn, an ancient name of uncertain derivation. It probably goes back to the Old Celtic name Lugobelinos, the first element of which is Lugu- (the name of a god; see Lleu ); the second is a name-forming element found also in names such as Cunobelinus (Cymbeline). The name was borne by Llywelyn ap Iorwerth ( 1173–1240 ) and his grandson Llywelyn ap Gruffydd ( d. 1282 ), Welsh princes who for a time united their countrymen in North Wales and led opposition to the power of the Norman barons in South Wales and the Marches.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

en.lifeSummary: When Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn was born in 1196, in Tregynon, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd, was 37 and his mother, Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain, was 40. He married Alswn verch Maredudd about 1240, in Montgomeryshire, Wales. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 6 daughters.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn est né en 1196, à Tregynon, Montgomeryshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Alswn verch Maredudd environ 1240, à Montgomeryshire, Galles. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 6 filles.

de.lifeSummary: Als Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn 1196 in Tregynon, Montgomeryshire, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd 37 und seine Mutter Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain 40. Er hat um 1240 in Montgomeryshire, Wales Alswn verch Maredudd geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 6 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn è nato il nel 1196 nel Tregynon, Montgomeryshire, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd, aveva 37 anni e il suo madre, Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain, aveva 40 anni. Lui sposato Alswn verch Maredudd circa 1240, nel Montgomeryshire, Galles. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 6 figlie.

ja.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwynが1196年にイギリスウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアトレグイノンで生まれた時，父Meilir Gryg ap Gruffuddは37歳，母Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewainは40歳であった。約1240年にウェールズモントゴメリシュアイアでAlswn verch Mareduddと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と6人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn(이)가 1196년 영국 웨일스 몽고머이실르 트레그이넌에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain(은)는 40세였다. 그는 대략 1240년 웨일스 몽고머이실르에서 Alswn verch Maredudd(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn nasceu em 1196, em Tregynon, Montgomeryshire, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Alswn verch Maredudd aproximadamente 1240, em Montgomeryshire, Gales. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 6 filhas.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn родился в 1196 г. в местности трегинон, монтгомиришир, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Alswn verch Maredudd около 1240 г. в местности монтгомиришир, Уэльс. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 6 дочерей.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Llewellyn ap Meilir Gryg of Llwyn nació en 1196, en Tregynon, Montgomeryshire, Gales, Reino Unido, su papá, Meilir Gryg ap Gruffudd, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Dyddgu 'Jane' verch Rotpert of Cedewain, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Alswn verch Maredudd aproximadamente 1240, en Montgomeryshire, Gales. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 6 hijas.
